gaps: Most PokeAPI tooling exposes endpoint lookups, not Pokemon-centered workflows.
Agents need composed profile, evolution, move, matchup, and team coverage commands instead of manually joining species, evolution-chain, type, and move resources.
patterns: Pokemon users think in named Pokemon and battle relationships, not raw REST resource paths.
Useful PokeAPI clients hide graph traversal across species, evolution chains, moves, and types.

novel_features.name: Pokemon profile
novel_features.description: Build an agent-ready Pokemon profile by combining core pokemon data, species metadata, type names, abilities, stats, and move counts.
novel_features.rationale: A single profile command saves agents from stitching together the most common PokeAPI resources by hand.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a user asks what a Pokemon is, what it does, or needs a compact structured summary.

novel_features.name: Pokemon evolution path
novel_features.description: Resolve a Pokemon's species and evolution chain into a readable evolution path.
novel_features.rationale: Evolution requires traversing species to an evolution-chain URL and then recursively flattening chain links.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a user asks what a Pokemon evolves into or from.

novel_features.name: Pokemon matchups
novel_features.description: Summarize type weaknesses, resistances, immunities, and offensive coverage for a Pokemon.
novel_features.rationale: Type matchup analysis requires loading each type relation and combining damage multipliers.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for battle planning, weakness analysis, and type coverage questions.

novel_features.name: Pokemon moves
novel_features.description: List and filter a Pokemon's moves by learn method, version group, and level learned.
novel_features.rationale: The raw pokemon endpoint nests move version details; agents need a flattened move list.
novel_features.example: pokeapi-pp-cli pokemon moves bulbasaur --method level-up --version-group red-blue --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a user asks what moves a Pokemon learns and how.

novel_features.name: Team coverage
novel_features.description: Analyze a comma-separated Pokemon team for shared weaknesses, resistances, immunities, and offensive type coverage.
novel_features.rationale: Team analysis compounds individual type relations into a roster-level view, which raw endpoints do not provide.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a user asks whether a team is balanced or has dangerous shared weaknesses.

narrative.headline: PokeAPI as an agent-ready Pokemon knowledge graph, not just endpoint wrappers.
narrative.value_prop: This CLI keeps the full official PokeAPI REST surface while adding graph commands for the workflows people actually ask about: profiles, evolutions, moves, matchups, and team coverage. It is public-API friendly and requires no authentication for normal reads.
narrative.auth_narrative: PokeAPI is publicly readable. No API key is required for the generated read commands or graph workflows.
narrative.when_to_use: Use PokeAPI when a user asks about Pokemon, moves, evolutions, types, or team composition. Prefer the graph commands for common questions; drop to v2 endpoint commands when you need raw API resources.

recipes.title: Build a Pokemon profile
recipes.explanation: Combines core Pokemon fields into a compact agent-readable summary.

recipes.title: Check battle matchups
recipes.explanation: Combines type damage relations into weaknesses, resistances, and offensive coverage.

trigger_phrases: what moves can this pokemon learn
